behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser-to-handler handoff: markdown-heavy content can be valid JSON yet mishandled after parse (escaping, field propagation). A pure Unit parser test can miss cross-module regressions in normal hook input flow. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real process/OS I/O; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the hook input pipeline in-process across parser + normal handler with fixtures containing code fences, backticks, and `$vars` inside valid JSON strings; assert parse success and successful downstream handling state.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: This validates interaction between parse failure detection and trace-record construction (including diagnostic context), which is a module boundary. Unit-only checks on parser error objects can miss trace-shaping bugs. Rejected System because behavior does not require real stdin/process lifecycle; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output dependency exists.
behaviors.test_description: Feed malformed JSON payloads into in-process parser+trace pipeline and assert emitted `json_parse_failed` record includes required context fields (e.g., error location/snippet metadata) for diagnosis.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says "local error-policy path," indicating a local mapping function/object boundary without required external I/O. Real failure is incorrect mapping/no-throw contract and telemetry intent flagging logic. Rejected Integration because no concrete cross-module handoff is required by the behavior text; rejected System/Agentic/Workflow due no OS/LLM/multi-agent dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy mapper with a parse-failure error object and assert it returns handled result, does not throw, and sets telemetry-intent marker.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real subprocess/stdin/exit behavior and signal emission timing. In-process fakes can hide crash/termination-order bugs. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires "real process execution" and malformed stdin handling at OS process boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook as a real subprocess, pipe malformed stdin, assert non-crashing handled exit behavior and presence of expected parse-failure trace signal before process exit.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets real invocation path including stdin boundary and actual trace sink artifact creation, which is OS/process/filesystem/subprocess territory. Mocking can miss integration bugs in real wiring and artifact emission. Rejected Integration due concrete requirement: "real hook invocation path."
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin and verify the trace artifact in the real sink contains the expected parse-failure signal.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: This is a differential behavior across parser + error classification/trace paths using two fixture classes; failure appears when modules jointly misattribute cause. Unit parser tests alone may not catch attribution errors. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real process/OS boundaries; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run paired in-process cases through parsing/classification/trace path: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings, (b) malformed JSON. Assert only (b) yields parse-failure attribution and (a) follows success path.
